subject: Re: Warp 1.1.0 beta
body:
Hi everyone,

The Warp 1.1.0 beta installer is no longer available via the Dropbox link 
originally provided by Dimitry. Could anyone please share the installer?

Thank you very much in advance and best,

Florian

chrisv...@gmail.com schrieb am Freitag, 3. Februar 2023 um 15:50:45 UTC+1:

> Hi Dimitry,
>
> I was curious to try noise2tomo in the Warp 1.1.0 beta version. I tried 
> running it once to see the commandline input options and it didn't return 
> any. Instead it gave an error, asking for tilt series input. While trying 
> to run it a second time (with and without input of tomograms or 
> tilt-series), I now consistely get the windows pop-up screen stating: "This 
> app can't run on your PC. To find a version for your PC, check with the 
> software publisher." Noise2map still runs fine. 
>
> Do you know how to overcome this so to give it another go?
>
> Best wishes,
>
> Chris
>
>
>
> On Monday, 24 October 2022 at 21:25:55 UTC+1 hami...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Dimitry,
>>
>> Thank you for your continued support. When I decouple the raw data and 
>> processing directories, I noticed that it breaks the IMOD stack generation. 
>> I get no movies found for ... or none has been processed in Warp yet. After 
>> copying the processing folder into the frames folder (and removing the 
>> processing folder in GUI) the problem got resolved.
>>
>> Best,
>> Hamid
>>
>> On Saturday, September 10, 2022 at 12:49:58 AM UTC-7 teg...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Ah, I forgot what might be the most surprising change: You can decouple 
>>> the raw data and processing directories now.
>>>
>>> On Saturday, September 10, 2022 at 12:44:36 AM UTC-7 teg...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hey Warp Fam,
>>>>
>>>> It's been a while. 
>>>>
>>>> Here is my first attempt at the long-overdue 1.1.0 beta: 
>>>> https://www.dropbox.com/s/9w59sdwawo32ux2/Warp_110_beta1.zip?dl=0
>>>>
>>>> Most importantly, this version supports the latest Nvidia GPUs because 
>>>> it uses PyTorch for everything ML-related. Unfortunately, this breaks all 
>>>> backward-compatibility with previously trained BoxNet and denoiser models. 
>>>> The latter also includes M species, which you can quickly recreate by 
>>>> reusing the half-maps, mask, and particles from the last iteration of the 
>>>> old species. On the bright side, you can finally use fast GPUs with a lot 
>>>> of memory.
>>>>
>>>> M finally supports Relion 3.1-style STAR files, which should make 
>>>> particle import less annoying. No support for Relion 4.0 – I hope 4.1 
>>>> finally adopts particle tilt series, which might lead to further format 
>>>> changes soon.
>>>>
>>>> Apart from that, you won't notice many changes. Small tweaks here and 
>>>> there, bug fixes, probably some new bugs.
>>>>
>>>> Still to come in a future beta are command line versions of Warp and M 
>>>> for Linux, and a properly debugged Noise2Tomo, which is an improved take on 
>>>> IsoNet's wonderful idea. I also have yet to write a tutorial for 
>>>> Noise2Tomo, but you might be able to figure it out from the command line 
>>>> options. You need to denoise the tomograms with Noise2Map first, keeping 
>>>> the default folder structure.
>>>>
>>>> I hope to see some of you at the Tomo Congress tomorrow.
>>>>
>>>> Sorry for the delay,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>
